ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-26-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-05-2021 Continued To Apr-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Marcia Chun, via ZOOM, in propia persona.

At the outset, Ms. Chun advises that she attempted to electronically file her petition but that it had been rejected. After further discussion, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to APR-26-2021.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-01-2021 Continued To Apr-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Marcia Lynn Chun in propria persona.

Ms. Chun stated that she was waiting to receive her papers from her document preparers, People's Choice, which arrived on Feb. 3, 2021, then she sent the documents to her siblings in Hawaii for signatures, that when she reviewed the documents, she found some errors (some beneficiaries listed twice), that she tried to file over the counter on Feb. 10, 2021 but was informed there were more errors as well as mandatory e-filing requirement, that she sent the documents to People's Choice on Feb. 17, 2021 to help her e-file, that she was informed by People's Choice she had to pay them an additional $600.00 to correct the errors if she wants to continue with them, that she already paid People's Choice $4,000.00 so she asked them to return the documents back to her, that she received her papers from People's Choice on Feb. 26, 2021 with a note that the petition is incorrect as-is but no additional information telling her what is wrong, that she spoke with an attorney who is helping her with a trust and is willing to help her e-file these documents without charge. The Court noted Ms. Chun also has the option to set up her own File and Serve account to e-file. Ms. Chun asked if it's okay that the beneficiary's address has changed. The court noted that the important thing is for the beneficiaries to receive notice.

The status on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report on status of administration is continued to April 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-01-2021 Continued To Mar-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marcia Chun appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Ms. Chun stated that she received a letter from the State (Franchise Tax Board) indicating that no outstanding taxes are due and a letter from the Department of Health Care Services, but she has not received a letter from the Internal Revenue Services. Ms. Chun also stated that her Mother was told by her CPA six years ago that she didn't have to file tax returns anymore, that she has contacted the Federal tax services department, but they won't do anything over the phone, and that they do appointments, but she has been in quarantine. In addition, Ms. Chun stated that her document preparer said that they could the documents back to her on February 3rd. Status on close of estate is continued to March 1, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-04-2021 Continued To Feb-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marcia Chun in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Ms. Chun that a document preparer is not allowed to give legal advice, explained that there are complicated requirements and procedures in probate, that her roll as Administrator is different from a beneficiary and asked Ms. Chun if she knows what the next step is. Ms. Chun stated that she looked up a list on line as to what the duties are and that she believes the next step is to change the deed on the property to remove her Mother's name. The Court informed Ms. Chun that changing the deed is done at the end by a Court order, that she needs to file an inventory and appraisal within four months, that a Probate Referee will be appointed and asks what the assets are. Ms. Chun stated that there is the real property, clothing, some jewelry and a stock account in the amount of $2,500.00. The Court informed Ms. Chun that it will give her limited authority with more Court supervision. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary appointing Marcia Chun as the Executor with limited authority (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for November 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for January 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
